dimensions.rationale: US formulary access for EGFR Exon 20 insertion is established but 1L combination (Rybrevant + lazertinib vs. osimertinib) positioning faces significant headwinds. NICE review is still pending as of April 2026, creating UK access uncertainty and signaling potential global HTA pushback on incremental cost vs. osimertinib, which is well-entrenched and approaching generic timelines. Community oncology access is the critical gap: many community sites default to osimertinib monotherapy and lack motivation to switch to a bispecific combo. Companion diagnostic reimbursement for EGFR Exon 20 insertion testing remains inconsistent across commercial payers. CMS Part B buy-and-bill economics for IV Rybrevant are workable but ASP monitoring is required given IRA exposure risk. HEOR dossier vs. osimertinib must lead with OS data from MARIPOSA when mature; PFS alone will not move cost-effectiveness models past NICE or US payer thresholds.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Accelerate NICE submission with updated MARIPOSA OS interim analysis by Q3 2026. Deploy health economics field team to top 25 US community oncology networks with budget impact models showing 1L combo value vs. osimertinib sequential therapy. Secure at least 3 major PBM coverage decisions for 1L combination by end of Q2 2026. Owner: Market Access. Urgency: Immediate. KPI: Unrestricted formulary access at 70%+ of top 50 US payers by month 6 post-1L launch.

dimensions.rationale: PAPILLON data (Exon 20 insertion) is well-disseminated and established Rybrevant as standard of care in this niche. MARIPOSA data (1L broader EGFR) is the franchise-defining dataset and dissemination is underway, but penetration into community oncology practice is lagging academic centers. MSL team is deployed but oncology MSL bandwidth is stretched across Darzalex, Carvykti, and Rybrevant — there is a risk of insufficient call frequency on high-value 1L EGFR prescribers. Molecular testing advocacy is active but needs to shift from awareness to actionable reflex testing implementation at the pathology lab level. Pulmonologist engagement is a smart play given that ~30% of NSCLC patients present first to pulmonologists, but this is early stage. KOL network for EGFR-mutated NSCLC is well-mapped; key opinion leaders at ASCO, ESMO, and IASLC are engaged.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Dedicate 8 MSLs exclusively to Rybrevant 1L EGFR community oncology engagement by June 2026 — do not share these FTEs with hematology assets. Launch a medical education initiative with IASLC and ACCP targeting pulmonologists on EGFR mutation identification and referral pathways. Fund 3 additional investigator-sponsored studies on real-world molecular testing patterns to generate evidence for reflex testing adoption. Owner: Medical Affairs. Urgency: Immediate (MSL reallocation), Near-term (education initiatives). KPI: MSL reach to 80% of top 200 1L EGFR prescribers within 90 days; reflex EGFR testing rate increase from estimated 55% to 70% in target accounts by Q4 2026.

dimensions.rationale: Brand positioning is clinically strong: 'only approved bispecific targeting EGFR and MET' and 'chemo-free 1L regimen with superior PFS vs. osimertinib' are compelling, label-supported messages. However, the marketing challenge is translating a mechanistic story (bispecific antibody, dual EGFR/MET targeting) into a simple decision trigger for busy community oncologists who default to osimertinib. Current promotional materials skew too academic and do not adequately address the community oncologist's primary objection: 'Why switch from osimertinib when it works?' The 1L combination story must lead with patient outcomes, not mechanism. SC formulation messaging is premature until approval is confirmed but pre-launch buzz should begin. Competitive response plan for AstraZeneca's inevitable osimertinib defense campaign (head-to-head framing, real-world data drops) needs to be battle-tested. Patient-facing materials for EGFR-mutated NSCLC are adequate but do not address the biomarker testing journey clearly enough.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Redesign 1L EGFR promotional materials to lead with MARIPOSA PFS superiority data in a simple visual format optimized for 60-second rep interactions in community oncology offices. Develop and rehearse competitive response playbook for top 5 anticipated AstraZeneca counter-messages by end of May 2026. Create patient-facing 'Ask About Your EGFR Test' campaign to drive biomarker testing pull-through from the demand side. Owner: Marketing. Urgency: Immediate (promotional redesign), Near-term (competitive playbook). KPI: Aided brand recall among community oncologists increase from baseline to 65% within 6 months; share of voice parity with Tagrisso in 1L EGFR digital channels by Q3 2026.

dimensions.rationale: Field force is deployed and trained on Exon 20 insertion indication. 1L EGFR combination training is in progress but field confidence scores on MARIPOSA data storytelling are below threshold based on internal assessments — reps can recite the data but struggle with competitive objection handling vs. osimertinib. Territory alignment is appropriate for the academic/large community split but micro-targeting of high-potential 1L EGFR prescribers who currently default to osimertinib needs refinement. CRM data shows call plans are still weighted toward Exon 20 insertion accounts rather than the much larger 1L classical EGFR mutation opportunity. Hub and specialty pharmacy network for IV infusion is operational. SC launch will require new channel onboarding — this planning must begin now even if SC approval timeline is uncertain.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Conduct intensive 2-day competitive selling workshop for all Rybrevant-aligned reps focused exclusively on 1L EGFR objection handling vs. osimertinib — complete by end of May 2026. Rebalance call plans to shift 40% of effort toward top-decile 1L EGFR prescribers identified via IQVIA switching data. Initiate SC formulation channel readiness planning with specialty distributors even ahead of regulatory clarity. Owner: Commercial Operations. Urgency: Immediate (training and call plan rebalance). KPI: Field confidence score on 1L EGFR competitive selling above 4.0/5.0 by June 2026; 1L EGFR target account coverage at 85% within 60 days.

dimensions.rationale: FDA approval is in hand for both Exon 20 insertion and 1L EGFR combination. Label supports the three approved key messages without qualification. No REMS requirement — a significant advantage vs. CAR-T assets. EMA approval provides EU5 market access runway. SC formulation regulatory pathway is progressing; bridging data from subcutaneous bioequivalence studies is expected to support a supplemental BLA. No outstanding FDA post-marketing requirements that would constrain promotion. Safety profile (infusion-related reactions, rash, paronychia) is manageable and well-characterized in labeling. Risk: any SC formulation delay would compress the timeline for the next major commercial catalyst. NICE review outcome is the key regulatory/HTA unknown — an unfavorable determination would cascade into EU5 payer negotiations.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Prepare two NICE submission scenarios (favorable with restrictions, unfavorable) with pre-built market access contingency plans for each by Q3 2026. Ensure SC sBLA submission timeline is tracked weekly at VP level with immediate escalation protocol for any CMC or clinical hold signals. Maintain promotional review committee bandwidth to turn SC launch materials within 15 business days of approval. Owner: Regulatory. Urgency: Near-term (NICE scenarios), Strategic (SC sBLA tracking). KPI: SC sBLA submission on or ahead of projected timeline; NICE decision response materials deployed within 48 hours of announcement.

dimensions.rationale: Patient support hub is operational for IV Rybrevant with copay assistance and prior authorization support. However, the 1L EGFR combination introduces a two-drug regimen (Rybrevant + lazertinib) which creates patient confusion about copay stacking, adherence to both IV and oral components, and navigation complexity. Current hub is not yet optimized for combination therapy support — intake workflows still treat each drug independently. Infusion site coordination is adequate at academic centers but community oncology infusion suites report longer scheduling lead times for the initial IV loading doses. SC formulation, when approved, will fundamentally change the patient support model and requires parallel planning. Patient advocacy engagement with LUNGevity and EGFR Resisters is active but could be deeper — these groups are influential in the EGFR-mutated NSCLC community and drive peer-to-peer support that accelerates treatment initiation.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Redesign hub intake workflow to support Rybrevant + lazertinib as a single combination regimen with unified copay, adherence, and PA support — deploy by June 2026. Partner with top 50 community oncology infusion sites to create Rybrevant scheduling templates that reduce time-to-first-infusion. Deepen partnership with EGFR Resisters and LUNGevity with co-developed patient education on biomarker testing and combination therapy expectations. Owner: Patient Support / Commercial Ops. Urgency: Immediate (hub redesign). KPI: Time from PA approval to first infusion under 10 business days at 80% of hub-enrolled patients; patient NPS for combination support experience above 60 by Q4 2026.

dimensions.rationale: Rybrevant's competitive position is strong on data but vulnerable on market inertia. Osimertinib (Tagrisso) is deeply entrenched as 1L standard of care with >80% market share in EGFR-mutated NSCLC. AstraZeneca will defend aggressively — expect real-world evidence campaigns, patient assistance sweeteners, and KOL saturation. MARIPOSA's PFS superiority vs. osimertinib is the most potent competitive weapon, but OS data immaturity is a known vulnerability that AstraZeneca will exploit ('wait for OS'). The bispecific mechanism and EGFR+MET dual targeting provide genuine differentiation but only matter if MET-driven resistance is positioned as a clear clinical concern at the time of 1L treatment selection. Erlotinib and afatinib are legacy competitors with minimal 1L share — not strategically relevant. Pipeline threats: amivantamab-lazertinib combo itself faces potential competition from next-generation EGFR degraders (e.g., BLU-451) in 2027-2028 timeframe. AstraZeneca's osimertinib + savolitinib combination is a direct competitive response to the MET resistance narrative. IQVIA data shows early 1L EGFR switching signals are positive in academic centers but negligible in community oncology — this is the battleground.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Launch a preemptive 'MET resistance matters at 1L' medical education campaign through peer-reviewed publications and CME by Q3 2026 to frame the clinical narrative before AstraZeneca can establish 'wait for OS' as the default objection. Deploy competitive intelligence war room with monthly IQVIA share tracking at the HCP level in top 100 1L EGFR accounts. Prepare OS data release communication plan with pre-approved materials for best-case, neutral, and adverse OS outcomes. Owner: Marketing (campaign), Commercial Ops (war room), Medical Affairs (OS communication). Urgency: Immediate. KPI: Community oncology 1L EGFR new patient share for Rybrevant+lazertinib reaches 15% by month 12 post-1L launch.

dimensions.rationale: IV Rybrevant supply chain is established and has scaled through the Exon 20 insertion launch without significant disruption. Biologic manufacturing at J&J's Leiden and Cork facilities provides geographic redundancy. Specialty distribution through McKesson and AmerisourceBergen is operational with adequate safety stock levels. The 1L EGFR indication expansion significantly increases volume demand — demand forecasting models must be validated against actual uptake curves. SC formulation introduces a new drug product requiring separate manufacturing, packaging, cold chain validation, and distributor onboarding. This is the primary supply/distribution risk: SC launch readiness depends on manufacturing scale-up that has a long lead time and cannot be compressed post-approval. No current drug shortage signals or raw material constraints flagged.
dimensions.gap_closing_action: Validate 1L EGFR demand forecast against first 90 days of actual prescription data and adjust manufacturing orders by July 2026 — overstock rather than risk stockout during critical launch window. Initiate SC formulation manufacturing validation batches and distributor onboarding NOW, parallel-pathing with regulatory timeline rather than waiting for approval. Establish 16-week safety stock target for both IV and SC formulations at all major specialty distributors. Owner: Supply Chain / Commercial Ops. Urgency: Near-term (demand validation), Immediate (SC manufacturing parallel-pathing). KPI: Zero stockout days at any top-20 specialty pharmacy in first 12 months; SC formulation available at 90% of distribution points within 30 days of approval.
